Mickelia hemiotis is native to tropical regions and is known for its unique and often epiphytic growth habit. The plant is often found in shaded and humid environments, where it can thrive in the absence of direct sunlight. As a member of the Polypodiaceae family, Mickelia hemiotis is part of a diverse group of ferns that are widely distributed across the globe.

intro: Mickelia hemiotis, also known as Piptoporus hemiotis, is a species of bracket fungus, specifically a type of polypore mushroom belonging to the genus Mickelia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Mickelia hemiotis include triterpenoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which have been shown to contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Mickelia hemiotis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture or capsule to support immune function and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
